sanskrit:
नैते सृती पार्थ जानन् योगी मुह्यति कश्चन।
तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन।।
transliteration:
naite sṛtī pārtha jānan yogī muhyati kaścana |
tasmāt sarveṣu kāleṣu yoga-yukto bhavārjuna ||
literal_meaning: na—never; ete—all these; sṛtī—paths; pārtha—O son of Pṛthā; jānan—knowing; yogī—the yogī; muhyati—is bewildered; kaścana—any; tasmāt—therefore; sarveṣu—in all; kāleṣu—times; yoga-yuktaḥ—engaged in yoga; bhava—be; arjuna—O Arjuna.
contextual_meaning: Knowing both the bright and dark paths the soul may travel at death, the steadfast yogī is never confused; therefore keep your mind continuously yoked to inner practice.
psychological_state: Post-decision calm after receiving a clear map for the ultimate transition; reduction of death-related cognitive load through single-pointed devotion.
trigger_scenario: A patient finishing advance-care planning who, once the forms are signed, feels an unexpected wave of peace because the uncertainty has been replaced by a chosen path.
anchor_text: Stay inwardly steady, knowing all paths, and keep your mind always joined to practice.
